“

You've seen the sign. Now come stay under it.

The red arrow has pointed the way to Drossos Motel since the Eisenhower era. Air-Conditioned. Cable T.V. Vacancy. The promises haven't changed much — the rooms beneath them have been reimagined for today.

60+ Years on Main Rd.
3 Generations family-run
14 Freshly renovated units
The Big House · Drossos Family Archive
The Drossos Legacy

A family business that grew one cottage at a time.

We are a family-owned and run hospitality and family-entertainment business — dedicated to quality accommodations, family-oriented fun, and delicious desserts. For well over 60 years we've been welcoming guests to the North Fork.

How it started

Grandparents Alexandra and John Drossos built the first units after a disastrous fire destroyed their original home. Over time the motel expanded to fourteen units. The mini-golf course and the ice cream stand were added as the years went on.

As John and Alexandra got older, their daughter Minnie and son-in-law Gus took over the reins and added food service to the ice cream stand. Gus has since passed, but the Drossos fried chicken he created is still legendary — one of the most-ordered items at the take-away window.

How it's going

Today, Minnie & Elaine welcome guests to the motel while overseeing the mini-golf course and snack bar. A couple of years back, Elaine partnered with family friend Jennie — a French-trained chef who basically grew up at the mini-golf alongside Kyle, Elaine's son — to launch Jennies at Drossos, a gourmet pop-up at the snack bar that has since become a full-blown culinary experience.

And last year, Elaine took on the motel itself: new flooring, modernized bathrooms, new furniture, new beds, new linens. Every room freshly painted. Work continues on the exterior. The bones are the same. The stay has never been better.

The Rooms

Refreshed for right now.

All fourteen units have been freshly renovated — new flooring, modernized bathrooms, fresh linens, and new furniture throughout. The soul of a 1960s roadside motel, the comfort of a 2026 stay.

Sleeps 2

The Queen

Crisp linens, warm coastal artwork, and a new queen bed framed by fresh paint and new flooring. Our most popular renovated unit.

View Room
Sleeps 2–3

The Coastal Queen

A queen bed with an extra daybed — comfortable for a small family or a couple who likes elbow-room. Ocean-inspired wall art throughout.

View Room
Sleeps 2

The Twin

Two twin beds, modernized bath, and plenty of natural light. Perfect for friends, siblings, or anyone who likes their own bed.

View Room
More Than a Motel

A whole little world on Main Road.

Come for the rooms. Stay for the miniature golf, the fried chicken at the snack bar, and the gourmet pop-up that happens to be run by a French-trained chef who grew up on the property.

No. 01

The Snack Bar.

Sundaes. Shakes. Cones. Hot dogs. And the Drossos fried chicken recipe that's been drawing people off Route 25 for decades. Open daily through the season.

Sundaes · Shakes · Cones
No. 02

Minature Golf, the original way.

A hand-built eighteen-hole course with wooden windmills, a little red schoolhouse, and all the mid-century charm of the first time you ever swung a putter. Included with your stay.

18 Holes · Family Friendly
No. 03

Jennies at Drossos.

A few summers ago, Elaine handed family-friend Jennie — a French-trained chef who grew up here — the keys to the snack bar kitchen. The pop-up that followed has become one of the North Fork's best-kept dinner reservations.

Gourmet · Seasonal · Reservations Recommended
On the Property

Sixty years of roadside charm, refreshed.

Where to Find Us

On Greenport's Main Road, just outside the village.

Drossos Motel sits on Route 25 / Main Road — minutes from Greenport Village, the North Ferry to Shelter Island, the wineries, the beaches, and everywhere on the North Fork you actually want to go.
